title: Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund Tests Constitutional Limits
summary: The deal the president reached with his own subordinates relies on a mechanism created by Congress that legal experts had warned was subject to manipulation.

title: Jan. 6 Police Officers Sue to Block Trump’s Payout Fund
summary: The two officers accused the administration of creating a “slush fund” to reward rioters and groups that committed violence on behalf of President Trump.

title: Prison to Pardons to Payouts: Jan. 6 Rioters Are Elated at Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
summary: The possibility that people who ransacked the Capitol could get money from the government they attacked is the latest head-spinning twist in President Trump’s effort to rewrite the history of Jan. 6.

title: Cuba Says the Trump Administration Is Not Negotiating in Good Faith
summary: Cuba’s ambassador to the United Nations said in an interview that Havana wanted to talk but the Trump administration was creating pretexts for military action.

title: Rubio, in Video, Urges Cubans to Align With Trump Administration
summary: In a rare direct address, Secretary of State Marco Rubio blamed the former leader Raúl Castro for the country’s longstanding electricity and resource shortages.

title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard-Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
summary: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.

title: Iran Threatens to Strike Beyond the Middle East if the U.S. Resumes Attacks
summary: The warning was issued as President Trump and Vice President JD Vance say progress is being made toward a deal, while keeping open the threat of renewed strikes.

title: Barney Frank, Gay Pioneer and Liberal Stalwart in Congress, Dies at 86
summary: Often voted the “brainiest,” “funniest” and “most eloquent” member of the House, he was also the first to come out voluntarily and helped normalize being openly gay in public office.

title: Former Congressman Barney Frank, Now in Hospice, Offers Democrats Frank Words of Advice
summary: Mr. Frank speaks about the missteps of the Democratic Party and his hope for its future. “Frankly, if I weren’t dying, people wouldn’t be paying as much attention.”

title: Timmy the Whale Got Stranded Off the German Coast. Then Things Got Weird.
summary: A humpback that became stuck in the Baltic seemed to unite a nation in hope. Rescue efforts followed, and then finger-pointing.

title: House Passes Housing Bill, Uniting on a Measure to Bring Down Costs
summary: The legislation, which had been stalled amid Republican divisions, passed overwhelmingly, signaling an eagerness in both parties to address affordability in an election year.

title: Prosecutor Is Charged With Stealing Sealed Report on Trump Documents Case
summary: The Justice Department accused Carmen Lineberger of emailing records related to the special counsel’s investigation to a personal account.

title: RFK Jr. Fires Leaders of Key Health Task Force
summary: The health secretary has undermined the work of the U.S. Preventive Services Task Force over the past year.

title: Jeff Bezos Praises Trump’s Second Term as ‘More Mature’
summary: The Amazon founder and owner of The Washington Post also rejected the idea that he has made changes at his companies to gain favor with the president.

title: OpenAI Prepares to File for an I.P.O. in Coming Weeks
summary: OpenAI would be one of the most highly anticipated potential initial public offerings, in what is set to be a major year of I.P.O.s for Silicon Valley.

title: To 2026 Graduates Facing a Bleak Job Market, 1991 Grads Have Some Advice
summary: The New York Times profiled young job seekers in 1991. Today, their own children are entering a work force in flux.

title: Meta Lays Off 8,000 Employees, As A.I. Casualties Mount
summary: Employees have signed petitions against being tracked by A.I. and were trying to figure out who had been let go on Wednesday, as the Silicon Valley giant tries to transform into an A.I.-first company.

title: Majority of Fed Officials Embraced Possibility of Higher Rates at Latest Meeting
summary: A record of the April meeting, Jerome H. Powell’s last as chair, underscored the extent to which the war with Iran has upended the economic outlook.

title: With Massie’s Primary Defeat in Kentucky, Trump’s Revenge Tour Continues. But Headwinds Loom.
summary: He has used his sway with his base to oust wayward Republicans more than he has made inroads with the independents his party needs to defeat Democrats in November.

title: Candidates Backed by Trump, Ocasio-Cortez Triumph in Key House Primaries
summary: The president’s preferred candidate ousted an incumbent in Kentucky, while a Democratic primary in Pennsylvania was a win for the democratic socialists.

title: Raffensperger Loses in Primary for Georgia Governor
summary: In his campaign for Georgia governor, Mr. Raffensperger found that G.O.P. voters still blamed him for Mr. Trump’s 2020 loss.

title: San Diego Mosque Shooting Follows Alarm About Rising Islamophobia
summary: Muslims pointed to a rise in overt hatred online, political attacks and harassment in the months before the killing of three people outside an Islamic center.

title: Ebola Outbreak Is Unlikely to Become Global Threat, W.H.O. Says
summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the World Health Organization’s director general, said the outbreak in central Africa was “not a pandemic emergency.”

title: Ebola Crisis Sparks Debate Over Global Health Double Standards
summary: To some Africans, the claim that the continent’s largest health agency had already bungled its response scratched a familiar wound.

title: James Murdoch Buys Half of Vox Media
summary: The media scion is buying Vox Media’s podcast network, New York magazine and Vox.com for more than $300 million.

title: Return to the Land, a Whites-Only Community, Is Sued for Discrimination
summary: Return to the Land, a 160-acre development requiring members be white and heterosexual, is breaking fair housing and civil rights laws, according to a lawsuit.

title: Arkansas Housing Development Restricted to White Residents Sparks Legal Concerns
summary: Housing rights experts say a community restricted to white residents is illegal, but the creators believe they could win a potential challenge in court in the current political climate.

title: A Founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics Gave Up the High Life to Be a Catholic Priest
summary: A mystical encounter 12 years ago led Scott Vincent Borba, a founder of e.l.f. Beauty, on a journey that will culminate this week, when he is ordained as a Roman Catholic priest.

title: Children of Radicals Write to Understand Their Legacies
summary: Zayd Ayers Dohrn and Harriet Clark on activism, violence, guilt and trying to make sense of their “incomprehensible” early days.

title: Book Review: ‘Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young,’ by Zayd Ayers Dohrn
summary: Zayd Ayers Dohrn’s parents were leaders of the Weather Underground. His new book traces how their revolutionary ideals collided with their family life.

title: Trump’s Slush Fund Will Reward Criminals. Americans Will Pay for It.
summary: The president’s corruption and subversion of democratic tradition risk becoming the norm.

title: E. Jean Carroll Taught Me to Not Be Afraid
summary: It’s not easy to make an anti-Trump documentary right now.

title: Harvard Caps A’s as Selective Colleges Attack Grade Inflation
summary: Faculty members overwhelmingly approved a limit on the number of top grades they can give to about a fifth of their undergraduate classes.

title: Video Shows Itamar Ben-Gvir Taunting Pro-Palestinian Activists
summary: The national security minister, Itamar Ben-Gvir, posted a video in which he is seen with handcuffed activists forced to kneel on the deck of a ship. It drew a rebuke from Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: After Eurovision Final, Dismay That Politics Didn’t Play a Bigger Role
summary: Politics isn’t supposed to influence the international song contest, but some people wanted relationships between countries to hold more sway in this year’s voting.

title: Surgeon General’s Office Issues Screen Time Warning
summary: The post of surgeon general has remained empty since President Trump took office last year.
